# Copilot Instructions for System 2 ML Lab

- **Stack**: Next.js 15 App Router with Turbopack (React 19), TypeScript, Prisma/PostgreSQL, CSS Modules. Bundle analyzer available via `ANALYZE=true npm run build`.
- **Core config**: Centralized site/SEO settings live in [src/lib/config.ts](src/lib/config.ts); domain changes are env-driven (see [DOMAIN-CHANGE-GUIDE.md](DOMAIN-CHANGE-GUIDE.md)). Metadata and PWA wiring are in [src/app/layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx) and structured data helpers in [src/components/StructuredData.tsx](src/components/StructuredData.tsx). Robots/sitemap use `siteConfig` ([src/app/robots.ts](src/app/robots.ts), [src/app/sitemap.ts](src/app/sitemap.ts)).
- **Runtime env**: Required envs `DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_NAME`; optional SEO/analytics handles (`NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_VERIFICATION`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_TWITTER_HANDLE`, etc.). `postinstall` runs `prisma generate`.
- **Dev workflows**: `npm run dev` (Turbopack), `npm run build` (prisma generate + next build), `npm run start`, `npm run lint`, `npm run db:seed` (via [prisma/seed.ts](prisma/seed.ts)).
- **Data model**: Prisma schema in [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma) defines `User`, `NewsItem`/`NewsLink`, `Page`/`Section`, `Person`, `Publication`, `Course`, `GalleryItem`, `SiteSettings` with indexes and unique constraints. Use these names/fields in Prisma queries.
- **Rendering**: Home and About pages are dynamic (`force-dynamic`) and fetch via Prisma ([src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx), [src/app/about/page.tsx](src/app/about/page.tsx)). About sections may render stored HTML; keep sanitization in mind if modifying.
- **Auth**: Custom JWT issued at [src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts](src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts) (bcrypt check, 30 min expiry) and stored in `auth-token` cookie. Verification lives in [src/lib/auth.ts](src/lib/auth.ts) using `jose`. `/admin` routes are gated by [src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) (redirects to `/admin/login`, clears bad cookies).
- **CSRF**: State-changing requests go through `requireAuth` which calls `requireCsrfProtection` (Origin/Referer allowlist built from env) in [src/lib/csrf.ts](src/lib/csrf.ts). Skip only when explicitly safe via `requireAuth(request, { skipCsrf: true })`.
- **Rate limiting**: In-memory limiters in [src/lib/ratelimit.ts](src/lib/ratelimit.ts) — login 5/15min, uploads 10/min, generic API 100/min. Preserve/extend limits on new endpoints.
- **Validation**: Use Zod schemas and `validateRequest` from [src/lib/validation.ts](src/lib/validation.ts) (covers auth, settings, people, publications, news, courses, sections, gallery). Match existing admin APIs.
- **Admin APIs**: Patterns: `requireAuth` + `validateRequest` + Prisma CRUD, returning JSON (e.g., [src/app/api/admin/news/route.ts](src/app/api/admin/news/route.ts)). Keep responses consistent and include relations via `include` as needed.
- **Public APIs**: Some endpoints (e.g., [src/app/api/news/route.ts](src/app/api/news/route.ts)) are open for reads; writes belong in admin namespaces with auth + validation.
- **Uploads**: Protected endpoints [src/app/api/upload/route.ts](src/app/api/upload/route.ts) and [src/app/api/upload/image/route.ts](src/app/api/upload/image/route.ts) apply auth, rate limits, and magic-byte validation ([src/lib/fileValidation.ts](src/lib/fileValidation.ts)); files land under `public/uploads` or `public/images/people` with sanitized filenames. Reuse helpers for new upload flows.
- **SEO/analytics**: OpenGraph/Twitter metadata are environment-driven; Structured Data scripts are injected via dedicated components. Preserve `metadataBase`, canonical handling, and analytics scripts (Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights) in layout.
- **Middleware-safe code**: Middleware/auth/CSRF utilities run in the Edge runtime; stick to Web APIs (no Node-only modules) there.
- **Front-end conventions**: Shared Navigation/Footer/JoinUs components and CSS modules per page. News carousel currently relies on existing client components in `components`; avoid reintroducing jQuery/Owl assets in `public/design` unless intentional.
- **Security posture**: Avoid logging tokens or secrets; keep `httpOnly`/`sameSite=lax` on cookies. Maintain disallow rules for admin in robots. Prefer `NextResponse.json` with clear status codes on errors.
- **Useful references**: Security & performance backlog in [TODO-CHECKLIST.md](TODO-CHECKLIST.md); SEO summary in [SEO-IMPROVEMENTS.md](SEO-IMPROVEMENTS.md); UI/UX roadmap in [UI_UX_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md](UI_UX_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md).
